name: Crab Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1 lb. fresh lump crabmeat (2 cups), picked over and patted dry
1 cup fresh breadcrumbs (about 3 slices of fresh or day-old bread)
1 large egg white, lightly beaten with a fork
1/3 cup finely diced red or green bell pepper
1 tsp. vegetable oil, preferably canola oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F.
2. In a large bowl, stir together crabmeat, fresh breadcrumbs, mayonnaise, egg white, lemon juice, scallions, bell peppers, parsley, Old Bay seasoning, pepper, and Tabasco.
3. Put the dry breadcrumbs in a shallow dish.
4. Form the crab mixture into six 1/2 inch thick patties. (The mixture will be very soft.)
5. Dredge the patties in the dry breadcrumbs, reshaping as necessary.
6. Brush oil evenly over the bottom of a heavy ovenproof skillet (or spray with Pam). Heat the skillet over medium-high heat.
7. Add the crab cakes and cook until the undersides are golden about 1 minute.
8. Carefully turn the crab cakes over and transfer the skillet to the oven. (If you do not have an ovenproof skillet, transfer the patties to a baking sheet.)
9. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, or until heated through.
10. Serve with lemon wedges. Serves 6.
Note: This recipe contains approximately 168 calories per serving and 5 grams of fat.
